levels.body: *Tears* are "the visible feature of weeping" and, hence, the symbol of "grief or sorrow" ("tear, *n*.1" 1.b.).
worksCited: "tear, *n*." *OED Online*. Oxford UP, Sept 2014. Web. September 7, 2019.

levels.body: The tears that are as pure, transparent, and innocent as the [[pure]] [[crystallines]] are the symbol of honest "toil[ing]" (1) and suffering during the mortal life [hyperlink to "tears" language 1]. See here for the different interpretations of [[work]].

For further information on the tear in literature you may consult chapter III.2. "Das Motiv des lebendigen Wassers in der Bibel und religiösen Texten", especially „Exkurs: Wasser- und Tränenpoesie in weltlicher Dichtung" as well as „Interpretation: The Weeper" (chapter III.4) in H*eilig öffentlich Geheimnis* by Inge Leimberg (1996).
worksCited: Leimberg, Inge. *Heilig öffentlich Geheimnis: Die geistliche Lyrik der englischen Frühaufklärung*, Waxmann, 1996. Anglistische Studien 11.
